data.title: Most shorted S&P 500 stocks in September
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) held its position as the most shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stock as a percentage of float in September.

As of Sep 30, 21.25% of Super Micro’s (SMCI) shares were sold short, compared to 15.66% at the end of August. Note that, SMCI executed a 10-for-1 stock split this month, joining a growing list of chipmakers that have split their stocks in recent times.

Despite a recent downturn triggered by a delay in filing its annual report and a disclosure by Hindenburg Research that it has short the company's stock, the tech firm’s shares have gained 67% year-to-date and 64% over the past 12 months.

Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) took the second spot, with traders selling 94.6M shares of WBA short as of Sep 30 - up 14.1% from a month ago. Shares of the drugstore operator rallied yesterday on the back of better-than-expected quarterly results and a plan to close 1,200 U.S. stores.

Charter Communications (CHTR) had a short interest of 12.48M shares, representing 12.92% of its float. This marks a 5.5% increase in short interest from the previous month.

Here are the top 20 most shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of September:

  • Super Micro Computer (SMCI) - Short interest as a % of float: 21.25%
  • Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) - Short interest as a % of float: 13.3%
  • Charter Communications Inc A (CHTR) - Short interest as a % of float: 12.92%
  • Intl Paper Co (IP) - Short interest as a % of float: 11.57%
  • Carmax (KMX) - Short interest as a % of float: 11.15%
  • Aptiv (APTV) - Short interest as a % of float: 11.02%
  • Dayforce (DAY) - Short interest as a % of float: 10.83%
  • Paramount Global Class B (PARA) - Short interest as a % of float: 10.68%
  • Enphase Energy Inc (ENPH) - Short interest as a % of float: 10.62%
  • Fox Corp A (FOXA) - Short interest as a % of float: 10.55%
  • Franklin Resources Inc (BEN) - Short interest as a % of float: 10.5%
  • Albemarle Corp (ALB) - Short interest as a % of float: 10.4%
  • Ralph Lauren (RL) - Short interest as a % of float: 9.37%
  • Moderna (MRNA) - Short interest as a % of float: 9.14%
  • Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) - Short interest as a % of float: 9.1%
  • Pool Corp (POOL) - Short interest as a % of float: 8.89%
  • Southwest Airlines Co (LUV) - Short interest as a % of float: 7.67%
  • Campbell Soup (CPB) - Short interest as a % of float: 7.61%
  • Best Buy Co (BBY) - Short interest as a % of float: 7.07%

data.title: Yum! Brands is downgraded at TD Cowen due to restaurant development risk
data.description:
Yum! Brands (NYSE:YUM) slipped in early trading on Wednesday after TD Cowen lowered its rating on the restaurant stock to Hold from Buy.

Analyst Andrew Charles and his team view Yum Brand's (YUM) risk/reward as balanced at the current share price. The firm sees a risk that restaurant unit development will miss consensus expectations in 2024-25, while the removal of Taco Bell breakfast at 1/3 to 2/3 of U.S. locations presents was noted to present a 1% to 2% comparable sales headwind that risks a negative sales revision. On September 24, Taco Bell allowed franchisees to opt out of breakfast before an eventual relaunch as early as 2026.

"We model 2024-25 EPS 1% & 2% below consensus; while we view consensus EPS as achievable via cost cuts, we do not expect this to be rewarded with multiple expansion," wrote Charles.

TD Cowen assigned a base case price target of $145 on the assumption restaurant development grows at 5% in 2024-2025 and G&A growth is flat.

Shares of Yum! Brands (YUM) were down 1.16% in premarket trading. The restaurant operator is lined up to report Q3 earnings on November 5.

data.title: LanzaTech awarded $3M contract for U.S. Department of Energy
data.description:
• LanzaTech Global (NASDAQ:LNZA) awarded $3 million by the U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Fossil Energy and Carbon Management as part of a broader $29 million investment program to advance its carbon management priorities.
  • Project ADAPT will primarily be funded by the FECM investment of $3 million and includes a Company funded cost share portion of approximately $0.8 million, totaling an estimated project cost of $3.8 million.
  • The majority of revenue is expected to be received and benefit financial results in 2025 and 2026.

data.title: Phoenix Motor to add electric transit vehicles to third University of California campus
data.description:
Phoenix Motor Inc. (NASDAQ:PEV) announced it is working on four all-electric transit buses for the University of California San Diego for delivery in early 2025.

The 35-foot electric buses from the company's PhoenixEV brand will be deployed to decarbonize the last mile transportation, reducing particulate emissions on campus and in the neighborhoods around the university. The new buses, which will bring the university fleet to 20% electric. UC San Diego has committed to decarbonization by 2045.

"UC San Diego is a leader in sustainability, and we are happy to help them achieve their decarbonization goal," says Denton Peng CEO of PhoenixEV.

"This project will mark the third University of California campus to deploy our zero-emission shuttles and transit buses," noted PhoenixEV CEO Denton Peng.

Shares of Phoenix Motor (PEV) closed at $0.77 on Tuesday, vs. the 52-week trading range of $0.32 to $1.79.

data.title: Interparfums' name change is approved by shareholders
data.description:
Shareholders with Inter Parfums (NASDAQ:IPAR) approved a name change of the company to Interparfums (IPAR). The effective date of the name change was October 7.

With its last earnings report, Interparfums (IPAR) said that while the global fragrance market remains vibrant with positive trends leading to strong demand for our products, sell-in continues to grow more slowly than sell-out, and the company was seeing continued challenges in Eastern Europe. "Given this dynamic backdrop and despite our record second quarter sales and strong earnings, we are maintaining our 2024 guidance of net sales of $1.45 billion, resulting in earnings per diluted share of $5.15," noted IPAR management.

Shares of Interparfums (IPAR) are down 17.9% on a year-to-date basis. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on IPAR is Hold, with a low factor grade for valuation holding down the overall score.

data.title: Parsons gets $25M Middle-East transportation contracts
data.description:
• Parsons (NYSE:PSN) awarded two contracts worth $25M to perform design engineering for a Middle East transportation customer.
  • With the same customer, Parsons (PSN) has won more than $90M in contract awards over the last 9 months.
  • Under these contracts, Parsons (PSN) will lead the design engineering and construction supervision of transportation infrastructure enhancements.
  • These wins are part of a broader strategic focus of the Gulf member states including Bahrain, Qatar, Abu Dhabi, Saudi Vision 2030, Oman Vision 2040, and Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan.
